Question Name: What level of cybersecurity governance maturity does your organization have?
Question Description: Cybersecurity governance maturity ranges from None, IT Involvement, BU Involvement, Information Technology and Business Unit Involvement to Board Involvement. The more senior management involved the more mature governance the firm has.

Question Name: What level of cyber risk reporting maturity does your organization have?
Question Description: Reporting cyber risk can be nonexistent, elementary with spreadsheets from Information Technology , or spreadsheets that are Business Unit focused, elevating to integrated data driven reporting and up to integration with the Enterprise Risk Management systems.

Question Name: What level of cyber risk management focus does your organization have?
Question Description: Most companies start with vulnerabilities, move up to incident focus and then integrate cyber risk and Enterprise Risk Management (ERM).

Question Name: What level of CISO reporting structure does your organization have?
Question Description: Most reporting is to the CIO, however as more roles are added the reporting shifts to COO, CFO or CRO and finally at the board level.

Question Name: What level of cybersecurity investments does your organization have?
Question Description: Companies invest to relieve their pain points.  The first hint of pain is from the compliance team, which moves to a more incident focused maturity, to an audit maturity and then to an integration focus.

Question Name: What level of cybersecurity strategy does your organization have?
Question Description: Companies start with a network focused stretch and then add layered tools, advancing into threat intelligence and business process automation coupled with AI.

Question Name: What level of cybersecurity team members does your organization have?
Question Description: Pen testing is where most companies begin the level of skills with their teams, moving to vulnerability scanning, then Security Operations team skills, afterwards supplementing their teams from outside specialty services and then bringing that skill set back in house.

Answer: Vulnerability Focused - Pen testing and SOC Team
Previous level with outsourced specialty teams
Previous level with teams moving back inhouse for specialty services

Question Name: What level of cybersecurity tools does your organization have?
Question Description: Firewalls, IDS and VMS are the first tools implemented, followed by SIEM, the SOC team (bule, red) and integrated with Cyber Risk tools.

Question Name: What level of vendor cyber risk management program does your organization have?
Question Description: Companies start with excel, add macros, then move to a Third Party Risk Management (TPRM) application and lastly integrate it with security tools.

Answer: Vendor risk applications Integrated with cybersecurity tools

Question Name: What level of cybersecurity decision making does your organization have?
Question Description: Cybersecurity decision making starts with a single manager, moves to a C-Level sign off, and then multiple C Level signoffs and lastly to the board.

Question Name: What type of cybersecurity lead does your organization have?
Question Description: Most companies begin their cyber security team with an IT person, then move into having a compliance manager in place, followed by a security manager and ultimately a CISO.

Question Name: What type of privacy lead does your organization have?
Question Description: Companies typically start using a CIO as a privacy lead then migrate to using a attorney afterwards as they mature they will use a CISO perhaps, and as they mature to the fullest capacity a Data Privacy or Chief Privacy Officer.

Question Name: What level of inherent risk does your organization have?
Question Description: As the company grows in size the organization's digital footprint increases the attack surface and the company assumes more inherent risk.

Question Name: What level of cloud cybersecurity program does your organization have?
Question Description: In the beginning most companies rely solely on the cloud service provider for their cloud security. As they realize that the cloud service is not fully responsible, they obtain a limited knowledge of their cloud security requirements which allows them to budget to hire staff to ensure the controls they own are in place.  Later they will mature to  having a full cloud service cybersecurity program in place.

Answer: Some staff for limited cloud security

Question Name: How does your company identify the amount of cyber insurance you need?
Question Description: Many companies start with no cybersecurity insurance, however most of their third parties may compel them to consider getting cyber insurance in order to stay on their vendor list. Some companies will ask their attorney to weigh in on the decision, others will go to the cyber insurance broker who will use comparable companies to suggest the proper limit, and lastly the more mature companies will do a cyber limits adequacy study.

Question Name: What level of digital asset management program does your organization have?
Question Description: Digital asset management programs typically start out with an excel based approach. Once companies have over 10 systems or vendors this becomes an unmanageable effort.  This is when they switch to a system based approach. As they mature, they understand that this approach needs to be integrated to provide a continuous update.  Later they understand that it needs to be integrated with the cyber risk management system.

Answer: Integrated Cyber Risk Management system based

Question Name: What level of compliance program does your organization have?
Question Description: Companies usually start with a check box compliance effort which is the minimal amount they need to do. They move into a more advanced level of compliance program where they're understanding that the checkbox approach does not work. Robust programs are integrating in their compliance with their cybersecurity program. Zealous efforts are where compliance is integrated in across cybersecurity and privacy and ending all the siloed efforts of their organization.

Answer: Medium - moving out of check box mentality
Zealous - integrating with cybersecurity & privacy teams

Question Name: What level of user accountability does your organization have?
Question Description: Companies typically have cyber security or privacy policies in place and then move into a organizational Phishing exercise with their users. There's a mature they add organizational training around cyber security regulation and risk and lastly they start to do regular tabletop exercises.

Answer: Cyber Policies, Phishing, Org Training & Table-Tops

Question Name: Are background checks done on employees and contractors?
Question Description: Provide the details of how your company performs background checks on all employees and contractors.

Question Name: What level of cybersecurity product posture does your organization have?
Question Description: Initially, companies tend to have little customization in their cyber security products. They then mature into a level of customization that allows them to have more specificity to their requirements. Evolving into highly customized and then innovative cybersecurity products.
